summary: Merged the old shell-based context generation workflow into tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py. The Python tool now discovers all src headers, writes build/ctx.h, preprocesses it with either mwcc or pcpp, optionally formats, colorizes, copies to clipboard, writes build/ctx.c, and supports quiet/no-file flags. The PR also adjusted headers so m2ctx can preprocess them more cleanly: removed src/MSL.h, renamed MSL math bit-access macros from __HI/__LO to MSL_HI/MSL_LO, and made stdarg.h handle M2CTX/non-MWERKS cases with placeholder types.
what_changed: Deleted tools/generate_context.sh and moved its responsibilities into tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py.
m2ctx.py now computes repo paths for src, build, build/ctx.h, and build/ctx.c, then generates a sorted include header containing every src/**/*.h as #include <relative/path>.

Added mwcc_import and pcpp_import paths. mwcc_import preserves the existing mwcceppc.exe preprocessing route with wine on non-Windows platforms; pcpp_import adds an optional pure-Python preprocessor route.
Added command-line flags to m2ctx.py: --quiet, --no-file, --clipboard, --colorize, --format, and --preprocessor.
Added requirements for pcpp, Pygments, and pyperclip to support optional preprocessing, terminal colorization, and clipboard copying.
Removed src/MSL.h, which had only included Runtime/platform.h and declared vsprintf with unk_t-style placeholder types.
Renamed MSL math macros __HI and __LO to MSL_HI and MSL_LO in src/MSL/math.h, with corresponding call-site updates in math_1.c and trigf.c.
Updated src/stdarg.h to include placeholder.h, to typedef va_list as UNK_T under M2CTX for non-MWERKS builds, and to move __builtin_va_info declaration into the non-MWERKS branch.
smart_moves: Centralizing context generation in Python removed duplicated shell orchestration and made the tool easier to extend with flags for output file writing, formatting, clipboard, colorization, and preprocessor choice.
Generating build/ctx.h from src.rglob('*.h') keeps the context include list synchronized with the repository instead of depending on a separate shell find/sed pipeline.
Keeping mwcc preprocessing as the default preserved compatibility with the compiler-specific context path while adding pcpp as an optional path for preserving preprocessor directives.
The M2CTX-specific stdarg fallback avoids relying on host/compiler va_list behavior when generating context, which is useful for decomp.me/mips_to_c context files.
Renaming __HI/__LO to MSL_HI/MSL_LO likely avoids double-underscore reserved-identifier issues and improves clarity that these are project/MSL helper macros.
decomp_lessons: Context-generation tooling should be able to consume the project headers as they exist, but headers may still need M2CTX-specific escape hatches for compiler builtins or types that cannot be represented cleanly in generated context.
When replacing ad hoc shell scripts, preserve the old observable workflow first: generate an include aggregation header, preprocess it, optionally format it, and write a build artifact.
Optional dependencies are useful for ergonomics but should be guarded by flags and local imports so the base path remains usable without importing every helper package.
For generated decomp contexts, a dedicated macro/type path such as M2CTX can be safer than trying to make host C/Python preprocessors understand all CodeWarrior builtins.
Avoid reserved C identifiers in project headers when possible; replacing names like __HI/__LO with project-prefixed MSL_HI/MSL_LO reduces conflicts during preprocessing.
naming_conventions: Use M2CTX as the feature macro for context-generation-specific header behavior.
Use project/system-prefixed macro names such as MSL_HI and MSL_LO instead of double-underscore helper names.
Generated context artifacts are named build/ctx.h for the generated include list and build/ctx.c for the preprocessed output.
m2ctx helper functions follow explicit names such as write_header, try_import, pcpp_import, and mwcc_import.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly diff or function matching tactic was directly shown; the PR is primarily about improving the context generation pipeline used by mips_to_c/decomp.me workflows.
The retained mwcc -E preprocessing route suggests that using the target compiler's preprocessor remains the default tactic for producing accurate context.

The optional pcpp path was added to preserve preprocessor directives, which can help when creating contexts for tools that benefit from seeing macro definitions rather than only fully expanded output.

review_feedback.comment: Will implement a more elegant version of this later that uses the API directly. Works really well as-is, though.
review_feedback.lesson: The pcpp subprocess path was accepted as a pragmatic implementation, with an acknowledged future improvement to use the pcpp API directly.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replaced pcpp subprocess usage with the pcpp API in tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py.
Search for further M2CTX-specific header guards or placeholder.h usage added after PR 726.
Check whether build/ctx.h and build/ctx.c became standard generated artifacts in later decomp tooling workflows.
Search for remaining double-underscore project macros that were renamed to avoid reserved identifiers.
